seniority.level: Group PM / Director-level IC or people manager
level_signals: 3+ years directly leading and managing Product Managers
Leads multiple squads including Trading, Account Experience, and Growth
Described as Group Product Manager with coaching and hiring responsibilities

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of product management experience, including 3+ years directly leading and managing Product Managers.
including 3+ years directly leading and managing Product Managers.
Proven track record leading the development of advanced brokerage or trading capabilities that require deep integration with execution systems, risk engines, and account-level controls.
Previous experience building world-class investing and brokerage products is a must.
Track record of hiring, developing, and retaining high-performing PM talent.
Experience defining and driving product strategy across multiple concurrent product areas.
Demonstrated ability to coach PMs on craft, stakeholder management, and career development.
Strong cross-functional collaboration skills, especially with engineering, risk, compliance, legal, operations, and analytics, to bring complex trading products to market.

Comfort working with systems involving real-time decisioning, account state, permissions, limits, and suitability controls, and understanding how these systems surface in customer-facing trading experiences.
Demonstrated ability to balance durable technical foundations with near-term product delivery, particularly when building capabilities that must scale safely over time.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate technical and regulatory complexity into clear product direction for diverse stakeholders.
Bachelor's degree or higher, ideally in a technical, finance, or quantitative field.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong technical fluency with APIs, distributed systems, event-driven architectures, and data-intensive services, with the ability to reason about performance, scalability, and failure modes.
Experience with brokerage clearing, self-directed trading, or wealth management products.
Experience with SQL, data exploration, product analytics tooling (e.g., Amplitude), and feature flagging / experimentation.
Experience at high-growth fintech companies, in a founder or GM capacity is a plus.
Experience building products in a highly regulated domain (e.g., fintech).
Experience leading complex, cross-team initiatives from ideation to execution at scale.

explicitly_excluded: Remote work from Hawaii or Alaska

scope.surface: SoFi Invest brokerage platform — self-directed trading, account experiences, new investing capabilities; squads include Trading, Account Experience, and Growth
scope.business_model: B2C fintech / national bank, investing and brokerage product
core_responsibilities: Own and drive the product vision and roadmap for Invest brokerage teams across Trading, Account Experience, and Growth squads
Hire, coach, and develop a team of Product Managers
Translate company objectives into actionable team goals and OKRs
Lead customer-focused product development from ideation to execution
Partner with engineering leadership on architectural decisions and technical trade-offs
Advocate for robust technical roadmaps balancing technical debt and future-flexible architectures
Improve product performance by analyzing launch results and metrics
Communicate product status, timelines, and blockers to stakeholders and leadership
Develop and improve PM processes, frameworks, and standards across the broader product organization

green_flags: Clear product ownership scope with named squads (Trading, Account Experience, Growth)
Explicit requirement for people leadership (not a hidden expectation)
Hard requirement for brokerage/investing experience signals a domain-serious team
SoFi is a national bank with scale — meaningful product impact
EEO and San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance mentioned — inclusive hiring signals
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed despite JD mentioning 'base pay range for this role is listed below' — range appears missing from the posting
Location type is unclear — no explicit city, remote, or hybrid policy stated (Hawaii/Alaska exclusion is the only geographic signal)
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned anywhere in the JD
Preferred qualifications list overlaps significantly with must-haves, making hard vs. soft classification ambiguous for some items (e.g., regulated domain experience)

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required, team scope, and Group PM title)
location.type (unclear; no explicit remote/hybrid/onsite policy stated beyond Hawaii/Alaska exclusion)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from SoFi's public profile as a B2C fintech/national bank)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from JD language 'based on individual factors such as... location')
equity_mentioned set to false (no mention of equity in JD)
ambiguities: JD states 'The base pay range for this role is listed below' but no range appears in the provided JD content — compensation may have been omitted or rendered incorrectly.

Location policy is entirely unstated. The only geographic constraint is 'unable to accommodate remote work from Hawaii or Alaska,' implying remote eligibility elsewhere, but no explicit remote/hybrid/onsite designation is given.

Several preferred qualifications (e.g., 'Experience building products in a highly regulated domain') closely mirror must-have language and could reasonably be classified as hard requirements. Treated as soft given their placement under 'Preferred qualifications.'

'Bachelor's degree or higher, ideally in a technical, finance, or quantitative field' — 'ideally' softens the field preference but the degree itself reads as a hard requirement; classified severity as hard for the credential itself.
